answer: Chalcatongo Mixtec distinguishes two copulas: 'kaa' for adjective predicates and 'kúu' for noun predicates. Word order varies by information structure: emphatic subjects use Subject-kaa/kúu-Predicate order, neutral statements use Predicate-Subject order without copula, and minimal forms drop the subject entirely. Nouns may take the definite prefix 'nde-' (nduča 'the water'). Adjectives can be nominalized with 'xa-' prefix to mean 'one who is X' (xasúčí 'young person' from súčí 'young'). Possessed nouns in predicate position use the pattern 'xa-ROOT-rí' for 'my X' (xalúlírí 'my child' from lúlí 'small'). The marker 'ii' appears to function as an indefinite article with kúu copula constructions.
